title: Behave
author: Robert Sapolsky
core_idea: Human behavior cannot be explained by any single factor — it emerges from a complex interplay of neuroscience, hormones, evolution, culture, and individual experience. Sapolsky takes us on a journey through the brain and beyond to understand why we do what we do.
key_concepts: Behavior is shaped by events from seconds to millions of years ago.
The prefrontal cortex is key to impulse control but matures late.
Hormones like testosterone and cortisol influence but don't determine behavior.
Us vs. Them thinking is deeply wired but can be overridden.
Free will is more limited than we like to believe.
notable_quote: We are not captains of our ships; our brains are.
practical_lessons: Context matters enormously — the same brain can produce vastly different behaviors.
Understanding the biology of behavior can increase compassion.
Change is possible, but it requires working with our biology, not against it.
Moral judgments should account for the complexity of human behavior.
